summary: Fix CardsGridItem card styling to match Figma design: add card border, image margin/inset, all-side rounded corners on image, and border around image

figmaAnalysis.observation: The Figma design (node 811:58775) shows cards with: 1) A visible darker stroke/border around the entire card, 2) Images that are inset from the card edges with visible padding/margin, 3) Images have rounded corners on ALL sides (not just top) since they float inside the card, 4) A subtle border around the image creates separation from the card background
figmaAnalysis.currentImplementation: Cards have no border, images extend to card edges with only top corners rounded, no image border
figmaAnalysis.colorContext: The design shows purple/lavender accent color cards. The border appears to be a slightly darker shade of the accent color or a semi-transparent charcoal.

changes.description: Add border to the main card container
changes.currentCode: <div className="w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col">
changes.newCode: <div className="w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col border border-charcoal/20">
changes.reasoning: The Figma design shows a visible stroke around the card. Using border-charcoal/20 provides a subtle border that works on any accent color background. The 20% opacity creates a soft but visible edge.

changes.description: Add padding to image container and change image border radius
changes.reasoning: The image needs margin from the card edges. Changing from a full-width container to one with padding creates the inset effect. Using p-3 (12px) on mobile and p-4 (16px) on tablet+ to match the design proportions.

changes.description: Update image container inner wrapper and image classes for TSX
changes.currentCode:
<ImageDropUploader
								image={ attributes.image }
								onSelect={ ( image ) =>
									setAttributes( { image } )
								}
								className="w-full! h-full! object-cover"
								imageClassName="w-full! h-full! object-cover rounded-t-2xl"
								placeholderClassName="w-full h-full"
							/>
changes.newCode: setAttributes( { image } ) } className="w-full! h-full! object-cover" imageClassName="w-full! h-full! object-cover" placeholderClassName="w-full h-full" />
changes.reasoning: 1) Wrapping the ImageDropUploader in a div with aspect ratio, overflow-hidden, rounded-xl (all corners), and border creates the floating image effect. 2) Changed from rounded-t-2xl to no rounded class on the image itself since the container handles it. 3) Added border-charcoal/20 to create the subtle image border seen in Figma.

changes.description: Add border to the main card container in PHP
changes.currentCode: <div class="w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col">
changes.newCode: <div class="w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col border border-charcoal/20">
changes.reasoning: Must match TSX change. Adding the same border class to maintain parity between editor and frontend.

changes.description: Update image container structure in PHP
changes.currentCode: 'w-full h-full object-cover rounded-t-2xl', 'style' => 'object-position: ' . theme_image_position( $image['focalPoint'] ?? null ) . ';', ] ); ?>
changes.newCode: 'w-full h-full object-cover', 'style' => 'object-position: ' . theme_image_position( $image['focalPoint'] ?? null ) . ';', ] ); ?>
changes.reasoning: Must match TSX structure exactly. 1) Outer div with p-3 md:p-4 creates image inset, 2) Inner div with aspect ratio, rounded-xl (all corners), overflow-hidden, and border creates the floating image effect, 3) Image class no longer needs rounded-t-2xl since container handles rounding.

before.cardContainer: w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col

after.cardContainer: w-full h-full bg-accent rounded-2xl overflow-hidden flex flex-col border border-charcoal/20

designDecisions.question: What border color should be used for the card?
designDecisions.recommendation: border-charcoal/20 - A 20% opacity charcoal border provides subtle definition that works across all accent colors (green, blue, purple, etc.). This creates contrast without being too harsh.

designDecisions.question: What border color should be used for the image?
designDecisions.recommendation: border-charcoal/20 - Same as card border for visual consistency. The slight separation from the accent background creates the 'floating' effect seen in Figma.

designDecisions.question: What padding should be used for image inset?
designDecisions.recommendation: p-3 (12px) on mobile, p-4 (16px) on tablet+. This provides enough spacing to show the card background around the image without making the image too small. The existing content padding is p-6/p-8, so using smaller padding for the image maintains visual hierarchy.

designDecisions.question: Should the image container change from rounded-2xl to rounded-xl?
designDecisions.recommendation: Yes - rounded-xl (12px) for the inset image versus rounded-2xl (16px) for the outer card creates a nice visual hierarchy and matches the Figma proportions where the inner element has slightly tighter corners.

testingNotes: Test with all accent colors (neon-green, blue, purple, orange, yellow, green) to ensure border visibility
Test in dark mode to ensure borders still work on charcoal background
Test at mobile (375px), tablet (768px), and desktop (1440px) breakpoints
Verify image aspect ratio is maintained after structural changes
Test cards without images - should still hide image area completely
Test focal point positioning still works correctly

rollbackPlan.description: If changes don't match design expectations, revert to previous implementation
